Title: MsAREB1 enhances combined cold and saline–alkali stress tolerance by promoting ascorbic acid biosynthesis in alfalfa

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis via the myo-inositol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes myo-inositol to D-glucuronic acid, contributing to the myo-inositol route for ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Described as a key enzyme in the MI pathway for AsA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges reactive oxygen species; supports tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly induced by combined cold and saline-alkali stress and by ABA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in MsMIOX2-overexpression lines; induced under CSA stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Combined cold and saline-alkali stress (0°C plus 200 mM saline-alkali mixture of NaHCO3:Na2CO3:NaCl:Na2SO4 = 2:1:2:1); ABA treatment (100 μM)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in alfalfa; enzyme activity assay; metabolite profiling; Y1H/EMSA/LUC for upstream transcriptional regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation and enhances tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress by reducing ROS damage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously named in Guo et al. 2024; directly activated by MsAREB1 and directly repressed by MsILR3 through the promoter G-box
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to raise AsA and improve tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress in alfalfa; may be stacked with positive regulators such as MsAREB1 while avoiding repression by MsILR3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsMIOX2 was significantly induced by combined cold and saline-alkali stress and ABA treatment. 2. MsMIOX2 overexpression increased MIOX activity and ascorbic acid content in alfalfa. 3. MsMIOX2 overexpression reduced ROS accumulation, membrane damage, and improved CSA stress tolerance. 4. MsMIOX2 is directly activated by MsAREB1 and repressed by MsILR3 through promoter G-box binding.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth defects reported under normal conditions

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AREB/ABF bZIP transcription factor with BRLZ domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis via transcriptional activation of the MI pathway gene MsMIOX2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates MsMIOX2 transcription by binding the G-box element in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: ABA- and CSA-induced positive regulator that promotes AsA accumulation under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a nuclear protein complex with MsILR3; antagonistic interaction reduces MsAREB1-induced activation of MsMIOX2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Combined cold and saline-alkali stress; ABA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges ROS; supports plant tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by combined cold and saline-alkali stress, peaking at 6 h; induced by ABA treatment; protein abundance increases under both treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Combined cold and saline-alkali stress; ABA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in alfalfa; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; protein abundance assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases AsA accumulation and enhances combined cold and saline-alkali stress tolerance by promoting MsMIOX2 expression and MIOX activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Has transcriptional activation activity with active region in the N-terminus; overexpression also upregulated multiple AsA biosynthesis-related genes, but direct evidence was strongest for MsMIOX2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsAREB1 is induced by combined cold and saline-alkali stress and ABA. 2. MsAREB1 overexpression increases AsA content and reduces ROS, membrane damage, and electrolyte leakage under stress. 3. MsAREB1 directly binds the MsMIOX2 promoter G-box and activates its transcription. 4. MsILR3 antagonizes MsAREB1 activity through interaction and promoter competition.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant phenotypic differences from WT under normal growth conditions were reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering alfalfa with enhanced AsA-mediated tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress; likely useful in stacking with MsMIOX2 or editing promoter networks to strengthen activation while limiting MsILR3 antagonism

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with HLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression and competitive promoter occupancy; antagonistic protein interaction with activator
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ascorbic acid biosynthesis via repression of MsMIOX2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly inhibits MsMIOX2 transcription by binding the G-box element in its promoter; hinders MsAREB1-mediated activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator that restrains AsA biosynthesis under non-stress conditions and is downregulated under CSA stress
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a nuclear protein complex with MsAREB1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Combined cold and saline-alkali stress; ABA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges ROS; supports tolerance to combined cold and saline-alkali stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly downregulated by combined cold and saline-alkali stress and ABA treatment; protein abundance gradually decreases under both treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Combined cold and saline-alkali stress; ABA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; BiFC; pull-down; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates MsMIOX2 transcription and antagonizes MsAREB1, thereby reducing AsA biosynthesis potential; its downregulation under stress relieves repression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MsAREB1 has stronger promoter binding than MsILR3 in competition assays; under CSA stress, reduced MsILR3 abundance favors MsAREB1-driven activation of MsMIOX2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsILR3 physically interacts with MsAREB1 in vivo and in vitro. 2. MsILR3 is downregulated by combined cold and saline-alkali stress and ABA. 3. MsILR3 directly binds the MsMIOX2 promoter G-box and represses its transcription. 4. MsILR3 competes with and antagonizes MsAREB1, modulating AsA biosynthesis under changing environments.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely repression of beneficial AsA accumulation under stress; no transgenic phenotype analysis reported for standalone overexpression or knockout in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for knockdown/editing to relieve repression of MsMIOX2 and boost AsA-mediated stress tolerance, but caution is needed because ILR3 family proteins often participate in broader metal, ROS, and developmental homeostasis
